title: radiogrammitis jagoriana
intro: Radiogrammitis jagoriana, also known as the jagor's grammitis, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Radiogrammitis jagoriana include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, with the leaves often being dried and crushed for later use.
